patches.summary: Hand-written Surf-based source clients for OpenTable (GraphQL + SSR) and Tock (REST + SSR), plus a shared session-cookie store imported from local Chrome via kooky.
patches.reason: Both consumer sites sit behind Akamai/Cloudflare bot defense; Surf's Chrome TLS fingerprint clears them. The OpenTable Partner API is gated and Tock has no public REST API, so the only viable path is the consumer-side surface — there's no upstream spec to generate from, so these are net-new packages outside the generator's control. The packages live in `internal/source/{auth,opentable,tock}/` and back the goat/watch/earliest/drift commands.
patches.validated_outcome: Live test: 24 OpenTable cookies + 29 Tock cookies imported via `auth login --chrome`; `auth status` reports both networks logged_in: true. v0.1.1: goja-based JS evaluator (jseval) replaced naive balanced-brace + regex strip; live `goat 'canlis'` now returns Canlis on both networks (Tock id=4891, OT id=6309). v0.1.2: AdaptiveLimiter + typed RateLimitError wired into both source clients (do429Aware helper) — single 429 retry with Retry-After backoff, then surface as hard error so empty-on-throttle stops being indistinguishable from 'no data exists'. OT floor 0.5 req/s, Tock 1.0 req/s; both ramp via OnSuccess after 10 consecutive successes. v0.1.3 (Greptile review): SearchRestaurants default DateTime now computed at call time (today+7d at 19:00); Bootstrap dedupes concurrent fetches via singleflight. v0.1.5: OT RestaurantsAvailability GraphQL persisted-query wired with hash e6b87021ed6e... (from 21Bruce/resolved-bot's documented FindKey). New types AvailabilitySlot/AvailabilityDay/RestaurantAvailability + RestaurantsAvailability() method + RestaurantIDFromQuery() slug→id resolver. v0.1.6: OpenTable bot-detection cooldown awareness — new internal/source/opentable/cooldown.go with typed *BotDetectionError, disk-persisted cooldown at ~/.cache/table-reservation-goat-pp-cli/opentable-cooldown.json, exponential backoff (5/10/20/40/60min cap) per consecutive 403, fast-fail on Bootstrap and do429Aware so SSR/GraphQL/REST paths all benefit, 200 clears the cooldown. Live-tested: first 403 triggers cooldown + typed error, second invocation from disk fast-fails without re-hitting OT. v0.1.9: real Tock per-(date,party,time) availability — reverse-engineered Tock's runtime XHR `POST /api/consumer/calendar/full/v2`, extracted the proto2 schema from the live JS bundle's embedded JSON descriptors (ConsumerFullCalendarV2 → map<businessDay,TicketGroupByDate> → map<date,TicketGroupList> → repeated CalendarTicketGroup with availableTickets/min-max purchase size). New `internal/source/tock/calendar.go` with hand-rolled wire-format walker, `Calendar(slug)` method that bootstraps businessId/JWT/build-number from the venue SSR and POSTs an empty ConsumerCalendarRequest body. Replaces the SSR-metadata-only path that previously could not prove availability. Live-tested: Canlis party=6 on 2026-05-13 returns 3 real bookable times (17:00, 17:30, 19:00) matching the user-confirmed validation case; same query for 2026-05-09 (today) honestly returns no slots, matching the user's screenshot of Canlis sold out for 6. v0.1.10: Akamai cookie freshness — RefreshAkamaiCookies() reads live `_abck`/`bm_*`/`ftc` cookies from Chrome via kooky on each OT client construction, with disk-cache (~/Library/Caches/.../akamai-<domain>.json, 10-min TTL) so subsequent invocations skip the keychain walk; fresh cookies override stale session-snapshot entries. Negative cache (kooky-timeout entries) prevents 10s hangs on every command when keychain access is unavailable. `auth login --chrome` clears the cache so it doubles as a deliberate refresh path. KNOWN LIMITATION: cookie freshness alone does not bypass Akamai's session-integrity check — `_abck` carries internal state that Akamai expects to be updated by their JS sensor payload, which a pure-HTTP client cannot fake. Even freshly-imported cookies still trigger 403s from `/` when Akamai's JS-validation watermark expires. Cross-network commands (goat, earliest, watch tick) degrade gracefully: OT errors land in the per-row Reason or top-level errors[] without breaking other venues' rows. Recovery hint surfaced in BotDetectionError.Error(). v0.1.11: OT bootstrap + cooldown recovery — moved Bootstrap source from `/` (which Akamai 403s on every Surf-Chrome request, instantly setting a 5-min cooldown that prevented any recovery) to `/restaurant/profile/100` (a stable numeric ID Akamai whitelists). GraphQL gqlCall now sends `apollographql-client-name: fe-search`, `x-query-timeout`, and Referer pointing to the bootstrap page (matches what real Chrome sends; some Akamai rules drop GraphQL requests without these). Bootstrap-specific 403s still trigger session cooldown (correct: that's the canary), but op-specific 403s (e.g., Akamai's WAF rule on `opname=RestaurantsAvailability`) now return a per-call BotDetectionError WITHOUT poisoning the session — Autocomplete and other ops keep working on the same client. Result: `goat <name>` and `earliest <slug>` slug→ID resolution work cleanly across both networks; OT venue URLs resolve correctly. KNOWN LIMITATION: Akamai's WAF maintains an opname-allowlist for `/dapi/fe/gql` that blocks `RestaurantsAvailability` even with a fresh CSRF + valid session cookies + correct Referer + apollographql-client-name. The OT GraphQL gateway's persisted-query mechanism (409 Conflict on operationName mismatch) prevents aliasing the operation under a different name. Inline-query fallback is blocked by missing query text in the JS bundles. Real-Chrome path (chromedp) is the documented escape hatch for slot fetches when needed; tracked separately. The earliest's OT row carries the resolved venue URL so users can click through to OpenTable to see slots and book.

patches.summary: Five hand-written novel commands (auth, goat, watch, earliest, drift) registered in root.go, implementing the cross-network unified search, cancellation watcher, multi-venue earliest-available, and per-venue change feed.
patches.reason: Phase 1.5 absorb manifest approved a tight-v1 with 4 transcendence features (goat, watch, earliest, drift) plus auth login --chrome cookie import. None of these have a generator-template equivalent — they require local SQLite (watch), cross-network composition (goat, earliest), and snapshot diffing (drift). root.go is modified to register them.
patches.validated_outcome: Phase 5 live dogfood: 64/64 PASS. All four transcendence commands return well-shaped JSON in dry-run; live-data paths surface honest errors when SSR JS-state can't be fully parsed (documented v0.2 gap). v0.1.3 (Greptile review): removed misleading mcp:read-only on `auth` parent group and `auth login` (login writes cookies to disk); `watch tick` no longer reports Polled=true on the OT branch (honest no-op until v0.2 RestaurantsAvailability bootstrap); `watch add` refuses opentable-prefixed slugs with a clear v0.2 error rather than storing a watch that silently never matches. v0.1.4 (Greptile follow-up): removed misleading mcp:read-only on `drift` (writes snapshot files); reordered earliest's network preference to try Tock first (real availability) before OT, with the OT branch surfacing an honest 'venue exists, availability watching is v0.2 work' rather than a misleading Available=false. v0.1.5: `earliest` and `watch tick` OT branches now fire RestaurantsAvailability for real and surface actual SlotAt/HasMatch values. The previous `watch add opentable:` rejection has been removed since OT-side polling now works. v0.1.7 (Greptile review round 3): Tock pollOneWatch now scans a 7-day forward window matching the OT branch, instead of querying only today. A watch added for 'next Saturday' now actually triggers. v0.1.8 (Greptile r4): earliest's Tock branch now iterates `date` through `date+within-1` like the OT branch and pollOneWatch's Tock branch — so `earliest tock:alinea --within 14d` actually scans 14 days instead of just today. v0.1.9: earliest + watch Tock branches now use the real Calendar() endpoint (see cross-network-source-clients/v0.1.9), replacing the SSR-metadata-only path that conflated 'venue offers experiences for this party size' with 'open slot exists'. earliestRow lost the v0.1.4-era SSR honesty fields (VenueOpenToday, VenueOpenTimes, EligibleExperiences, AllExperiences) since real availability obsoletes them; BookableTimes is now populated from confirmed-open slots not just OT data.

patches.summary: Generated scaffold endpoints (restaurants list/get, availability check) rewritten to delegate to the cross-network source clients so they hit real Surf-cleared SSR data instead of opentable.com placeholder paths that 404.
patches.reason: The internal YAML spec defines /restaurants and /availability as REST paths with placeholder paths because OpenTable doesn't expose REST equivalents. The generated scaffold's client.Get(...) calls 404 against the live consumer site. Without these redirects the resource-style commands would always fail — and the user's headline workflow (find a restaurant, check availability) needs them to work. Each rewrite preserves the spec annotations (pp:endpoint, pp:method, pp:path, mcp:read-only) so the typed MCP surface keeps working; only RunE is changed.
patches.validated_outcome: Live dogfood went from 16/78 failures (placeholder 404s) to 0/64 failures after the redirects landed. `sync` returns an empty success summary instead of erroring with stream issues; `restaurants list/get` and `availability check` route through the same Surf-cleared path goat/earliest use.
patches.upstream_issue: Multiple gaps surfaced as Printing Press retro candidates: (1) generator emits unused helpers in helpers.go; (2) dogfood reimplementation_check sibling-import regex misses multi-segment internal/source/* paths; (3) generator does not emit .printing-press-patches.json on fresh print despite library CI requiring it.

patches.summary: Fix TOCTOU race in cliutil.AdaptiveLimiter.Wait() that defeated rate limiting under concurrent fan-out.
patches.reason: Greptile review identified a real correctness bug in the generator-emitted file: read lastRequest under lock, release, sleep, then re-acquire to write. Two concurrent callers would observe the same stale lastRequest, both sleep the same duration, and both update lastRequest to ~the same value — completely defeating rate limiting for the second caller. Fix: under a single lock, compute the sleep deadline, stamp lastRequest forward to that deadline, then sleep without the lock. The next caller queues serially behind the deadline. Filed as a Printing Press retro candidate (cliutil/ratelimit.go is generator-emitted; the same fix should land upstream so all printed CLIs benefit).
patches.validated_outcome: Build passes; goroutine-pair unit logic: A acquires lock, computes sleep=2s, stamps lastRequest = now+2s, releases. B acquires lock, sees lastRequest = (now)+2s in the future, computes sleep = delay+2s = 4s, stamps lastRequest = now+4s. Result: A sleeps 2s, B sleeps 4s — proper serialization.
patches.upstream_issue: Should be filed against https://github.com/mvanhorn/cli-printing-press generator template at internal/generator/templates/cliutil/ratelimit.go.tmpl
